No Poverty.reason: Course discusses economic aspects of insurance, indirectly related to poverty alleviation.

Quality Education.reason: Course aims to equip students with legal research skills, linking to Target 4.4.

Gender Equality.reason: Course includes understanding social responsibility, relevant to gender issues.
evidence: understand and identify ethical, socially responsible, and sustainability issues

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Course covers risk management, relevant to economic growth and job creation.
evidence: acquire advanced knowledge in risk management and insurance

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Course focuses on law, relevant to justice and legal frameworks.

No Poverty.reason: Insurance is a key mechanism for social protection (Target 1.3) and building resilience to shocks (Target 1.5), which is a core function of risk management.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: Directly addresses financial risk protection through the study of "Life Insurance" cases, which is central to achieving universal health coverage (Target 3.8).
evidence: Metropolitan Life Ins. Co. v. Conway

Quality Education.reason: The course provides tertiary education and "professional skills", contributing generally to Targets 4.3 and 4.4, but its subject matter is not education itself.
evidence: acquire advanced knowledge in risk management and insurance

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The case study "In re Deepwater Horizon" directly relates to the legal and financial aftermath of major water pollution, linking to Target 6.3.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course is central to Target 8.10 by focusing on "Insurance law and regulation," which strengthens financial institutions and expands access to insurance services for all.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Insurance is crucial for de-risking and enabling resilient infrastructure (Target 9.1). The "World trade center properties" case is a direct example of this principle.
evidence: World trade center properties v. travelers indemnity co

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's focus on "Insurance law and regulation" directly supports Target 10.5 (improve regulation of financial institutions) and Target 10.4 (social protection policies).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Directly relates to building financial resilience to disasters (Target 11.5) through the study of "Property Insurance" and cases like "World trade center properties".
evidence: World trade center properties v. travelers indemnity co

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Links to corporate sustainability (Target 12.6) via the core competency on "sustainability issues" and addresses industrial environmental damage via the "Deepwater Horizon" case (Target 12.4).

Climate Action.reason: Insurance is a primary tool for strengthening financial resilience to climate-related disasters (Target 13.1). The study of property insurance and disaster cases is highly relevant.

Life Below Water.reason: The case "In re Deepwater Horizon" provides a direct analysis of the legal and insurance response to a major marine pollution event, linking to Target 14.1.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course is fundamentally about "Insurance Law and Cases," directly promoting the rule of law (Target 16.3) and developing accountable institutions through regulation (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The study of international (Anglo-American) insurance law contributes to global macroeconomic stability and policy coherence for sustainable development (Targets 17.13, 17.14).

Gender Equality.reasoning: The core competency to 'understand and identify ethical, socially responsible, and sustainability issues' provides an indirect link, as socially responsible practices can include non-discrimination and equal access to financial services.
